description: 6 West Germany Undertakes Study Os Nuclear Cargo Submarines BONN NANA The Bonn government is commissioning tech nical studies for a nuclear sub marine cargo ship which would operate independent of wind waves and ice and at speeds up to 50 knots The project is being organized by a committee representing the federal government the four state governments in North Germany with maritime interests and the iWest German shipbuilding in dustry The committee parallels that which is in charge of con struction of Germanys first nuc learpowered ship a tanker Details of the underwater freighter project were given in a lecture before the Bremen Nauti ecal Society by Capt Heinrich Giessler an officer of the Deutsche Gesellschaft Fuer Ortung Und Navigation German Navigation Society a participant in the study Capt Giesslers thesis was that the entire present concept of sur face sea transportation is out moded and that the logical oper ating area for nuclear maritime propulsion is subsurface Ex perts of Capt Giesslers school are convinced West Germany would be wasting its time and money to concentrate on nuclear surface ship propulsion 1 Hope For Breakthrough Instead they are campaign ing for the chanelling of all maritime nuclear research
